Her works included desert landscapes, cows' skulls, flowers, rocks, etc. She was a pioneering modernist, had her own vision, and viewed painting as a private endeavor. Her work was more American in its treatment of natural forms. So, O'Keeffe's relationship with nature is deeply personal and integral to her art. She uses nature as her subject and inspiration. She was influenced by the landscapes she encountered, especially the Western desert, and it became a major theme in her work.
